Dynamic Residency | 20-Year Exemption

Understand Türkiye's 20-Year Foreign Income Tax Exemption

For entrepreneurs, investors and internationally mobile families

Verified legal basis: Article 20/D of the Turkish Income Tax Law and General Communiqué No. 333

About Dynamic Residency
A New Tax Framework

For Internationally Connected Individuals

Türkiye has introduced a significant personal income-tax exemption for eligible individuals who establish Turkish tax residence.

Under Article 20/D of the Turkish Income Tax Law, individuals who satisfy the statutory residence-history conditions may receive an exemption from Turkish income tax on income and gains genuinely obtained outside Türkiye for a statutory period of 20 years.

The provision applies to individuals who become resident in Türkiye from 1 January 2026 onwards. It is based on residence history, tax history and the source of the income — not on nationality. The legislation does not state a monetary ceiling for qualifying foreign income.

Important distinction
This is not a general “20 years tax-free” status. The exemption applies only where the individual, the income and the application process satisfy the legal requirements.

Read Article 20/D in the Official Gazette

The Exemption at a Glance

Quick Facts

Who can qualify?Natural persons who become resident in Türkiye and satisfy the applicable residence and tax-history conditions. The regime does not provide a corporate tax exemption.
What may be exempt?Income and gains genuinely obtained outside Türkiye, subject to classification under Turkish source rules.
How long?A statutory exemption period of 20 years.
Is there a monetary ceiling?Article 20/D does not state a monetary ceiling for qualifying foreign income.
Is an application required?Yes. The individual must apply to the competent Turkish tax office and obtain the prescribed Foreign Income Exemption Certificate.
Potentially Relevant Foreign Income

Income Streams That May Be Relevant

Dividends from foreign companies

Rental income from property outside Türkiye

Foreign interest and investment returns

Certain foreign capital gains

Certain foreign pension receipts

Other income arising from assets or activities genuinely located outside Türkiye

Each income stream must be classified separately. A foreign payer, foreign company or foreign bank account does not by itself make income foreign-source.

Eligibility

The Four Questions That Determine Eligibility

01

Will You Become Resident in Türkiye?

Article 20/D applies to individuals who are considered resident in Türkiye under Turkish tax law. A residence permit, property purchase or number of days spent in Türkiye may be relevant, but no single fact should be assessed in isolation. Domicile, physical presence, family circumstances and applicable double-tax treaties may all affect the final residence position. The individual must already be considered resident in Türkiye when applying for the exemption certificate.

02

What Is Your Previous Three-Calendar-Year History?

As a general rule, the individual must not have had either a domicile in Türkiye or a disqualifying Turkish tax liability during the three calendar years preceding the year in which the individual becomes resident. Certain previous Turkish tax liabilities arising from Turkish rental income, investment income or capital gains do not automatically prevent qualification. Previous employment, commercial or professional activity in Türkiye may, however, affect eligibility and requires detailed review.

03

Where Was the Income Genuinely Obtained?

The location of the payer or bank account is not decisive. The underlying asset, activity, employment, service, company distribution or transaction must be examined to determine whether the income was genuinely obtained outside Türkiye.

04

Can You Obtain the Certificate on Time?

The exemption is not automatic. The application must generally be completed by the end of the calendar year in which the individual becomes resident in Türkiye. Individuals who become resident during the final two months of a calendar year may apply until the end of February of the following year. A late application can result in the exemption certificate being refused even where the other substantive conditions are satisfied.

Source Classification

Practical Examples

  • Rent from property physically located outside Türkiye may be foreign-source.
  • A dividend from a non-Turkish company may be foreign-source, subject to the surrounding facts.
  • Professional services physically performed from Türkiye may remain Turkish-source even if the client and payment account are abroad.
  • Salary, management fees and business profits may require additional analysis of where the work is performed and where the business is managed.
Why the Exemption May Be Significant

Who May Find It Relevant

For an eligible internationally connected individual, the exemption may substantially change the long-term economics of establishing residence in Türkiye.

International Investors

Individuals receiving dividends, portfolio income, interest, foreign rental income or gains from assets held across several jurisdictions.

Company Owners and Founders

Entrepreneurs retaining ownership in foreign companies after relocating to Türkiye. The personal exemption may be relevant to dividends or other qualifying foreign income, but it does not determine company residence, permanent establishment, salary taxation or departure-country exit tax.

Returning Turkish Citizens

Citizenship does not by itself prevent qualification. A returning Turkish citizen may be eligible where the previous three-calendar-year residence and tax-history requirements are satisfied.

Internationally Mobile Families

Families coordinating residence, investments, property, education, succession and long-term asset ownership across more than one country.

Retirees

Individuals receiving pensions, rental income or investment income from outside Türkiye, subject to the source rules and the relevant double-tax treaty.

What the 20-Year Exemption Does Not Do

Not a Blanket Tax Exemption

  • Exempt income arising from Turkish property, Turkish companies or activities performed in Türkiye
  • Exempt every payment received from a foreign customer, employer or bank account
  • Apply to companies or remove corporate tax obligations
  • Remove VAT, payroll, stamp-duty or social-security obligations
  • End tax residence or reporting obligations in another country
  • Eliminate departure tax or exit tax in the country being left
  • Prevent a foreign company from becoming managed or taxable in Türkiye
  • Override a double-tax treaty
  • Correct a late or unsupported exemption application

Foreign taxes paid on income falling within the Turkish exemption cannot be credited against Turkish income tax. Expenses and costs connected with exempt income also cannot be used to reduce income that remains taxable in Türkiye.

The Exemption Belongs to the Individual

Personal and Company Positions Must Be Analysed Separately

Article 20/D is a personal income-tax provision. A qualifying shareholder may potentially receive exempt foreign-source income while the company from which that income arises remains fully taxable in its own jurisdiction.

The individual's exemption does not automatically protect the company from:

  • Turkish corporate residence
  • Permanent-establishment exposure
  • Payroll obligations
  • Transfer-pricing rules
  • Withholding taxes
  • Local accounting and reporting requirements

For entrepreneurs and shareholders, the personal position and the company position must therefore be analysed separately.

Illustrative Profiles

How the Rule May Apply in Practice

Foreign Investment Income

An eligible individual becomes resident in Türkiye while continuing to receive dividends from a foreign company and rental income from property outside Türkiye. These income streams may fall within Article 20/D where they are genuinely foreign-source, the individual satisfies the previous three-calendar-year test and the exemption certificate is obtained on time.

International Entrepreneur

A founder relocates to Türkiye while retaining a foreign operating company. Foreign dividends may be relevant to the exemption, but salary, management activity, company residence, permanent establishment and departure-country exit tax must be examined separately.

Remote Professional

A consultant lives and performs services from Türkiye for clients located abroad. The foreign location of the clients or payment account does not automatically make the income foreign-source. Income connected with work physically performed in Türkiye may remain taxable in Türkiye.

Returning Turkish Citizen

A Turkish citizen returns after several complete calendar years abroad. The person may qualify if the statutory residence and tax-history conditions are satisfied. Citizenship is not the decisive test.

Application and Evidence

Plan the Certificate Process Before the Deadline

The exemption-certificate process should be planned before or during the relocation — not after the relevant deadline has passed.

The Turkish tax office may review:

  • The date on which the individual became resident in Türkiye
  • Residence and domicile during the preceding three calendar years
  • Previous Turkish tax registrations and income types
  • The nature and source of the foreign income
  • Supporting residence, tax and income documentation
  • The accuracy of the statements made in the application

If it is later determined that the conditions were not satisfied, unpaid tax may be assessed together with penalties and interest.

Official application guidance
General Communiqué No. 333 sets out the administrative application and documentation framework for Article 20/D.

Read General Communiqué No. 333 in the Official Gazette
The Wider Decision

The Turkish Exemption Is Only One Part of the Decision

A successful relocation must work in every relevant jurisdiction. Before changing residence, the complete structure should be reviewed, including:

  • Current and future tax residence
  • Exit tax and departure reporting
  • Foreign companies and shareholdings
  • Location of management and decision-making
  • Investment and property income
  • Employment and professional activity
  • Social-security exposure
  • Family residence and succession
  • Applicable double-tax treaties
  • Timing of the Turkish exemption application
The central question
The question is not simply whether Article 20/D exists. The question is whether it works for your income, companies, investments, family position and country of departure.

Important Information

General Information Only

This page provides general information about Article 20/D of the Turkish Income Tax Law and General Communiqué No. 333. It does not constitute individual tax, legal or investment advice.

Eligibility and income-source classification depend on the facts of each case. Regulated tax and legal opinions should be provided or reviewed by appropriately qualified professionals.

Introduced byLaw No. 7582
Administrative guidanceGeneral Communiqué No. 333
